name: ceratops_code_repo_nondeterministic_contract
description: Repository-content quality and intent checks that require AI-agent judgment.

notes: Collect a separate repository evidence bundle when live labels, Dependabot queues, alerts, or repository settings are also in scope.

review_result_values.pass: AI-agent review found the intent satisfied and recorded evidence.
review_result_values.fail: AI-agent review found a concrete mismatch.
review_result_values.approved_drift: The mismatch is allowed by the deterministic contract's approved drift profile.
review_result_values.blocked: Review needs an unavailable local checkout, missing product decision, or unavailable validation endpoint.
review_result_values.not_applicable: Repository type or source does not apply.

checks.applies_when: Public or internal content files are expected.
checks.review_required: Confirm README, license, security policy, contribution guide, code of conduct, examples, installation documentation, issue forms, pull-request templates, and support documentation are repository-specific rather than placeholders.

checks.applies_when: The repository has homepage, support, discussion, issue, or SUPPORT.md routes.
checks.review_required: Confirm support routing targets an active channel and does not conflict with issue templates or security reporting.
Confirm each workflow has a valid purpose, release workflows match artifact surfaces, full-SHA comments identify the intended release, and reusable-workflow tags are deliberate.
Confirm ecosystems, grouping, open-pull-request limits, private registries, labels, and update cadence fit the repository instead of only being syntactically valid.

checks.applies_when: Dirty Git state, generated files, local paths, or old automation references are present.
checks.review_required: Classify each item as active, intentionally retained, approved drift, stale, or blocked before cleanup.

checks.applies_when: Secret-like patterns are detected in local text.
checks.review_required: Classify each match as a real secret, safe placeholder, fixture, documentation example, approved drift, or blocked remediation.

checks.applies_when: Scripts, public APIs, automation helpers, contract checkers, or non-obvious safety logic are in scope.
checks.review_required: Apply the code-comment nondeterministic contract and confirm comments explain intent, safety boundaries, external side effects, and public usage without restating obvious code.

checks.applies_when: A repository-content convention or generated-file behavior matters and local evidence is ambiguous.
checks.review_required: Recheck only the relevant official documentation or local project standard before deciding drift.
